verdict_reasoning: SRC is typically anchored to the inner PM leaflet via N-myristoylation, making it a cytoplasmic/inner-leaflet protein at baseline. However, documented evidence from 2026 (Science) shows that SRC undergoes noncanonical surface inversion onto the outer plasma membrane specifically in cancer cells via autophagolysosomal exocytosis. This eSrc pool is present in cancer cell lines, xenografts, and primary patient tumors, but absent from healthy tissue. The protein is actively targeted by ADC, bispecific, and radioligand therapeutics on cancer cell surfaces. This qualifies as cell-state-induced surface display via its own lipidation anchor, not transient external recruitment.
